events.text: Day-to-day operations rely on reliable SSH connectivity. We start by verifying the tunnel to the remote machine -- a hostname check confirms the connection target.
We check uptime and load average over SSH to confirm the machine is responsive and handling its current workload well.
Now we connect directly into a repository's sandboxed environment. Running docker ps shows containers under that repository's own isolated daemon.
Before uploading files, a dry-run sync previews what would be transferred without writing anything. Here everything is already in sync.
Running the actual upload confirms zero files transferred -- the repository contents already match the local directory.
We list the repository's files to verify the contents, showing the application files alongside the internal configuration.
The VS Code integration check shows the installation status, Remote SSH extension, and all active remote connections managed by the CLI.
A quick update check reports available CLI versions without installing -- useful for staying current with the latest features.
